title: CoStar Group, Inc. (CSGP) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of CoStar Group, Inc (CSGP) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1. Economic Prospect Score: 72/100 — Moderate Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 15.0% growth rate reflects CoStar's incredibly strong, high-margin commercial real estate SaaS business and its massive ongoing expansion into the residential portal market with Homes.com. The transition of Homes.com from a heavy investment phase to monetization is expected to drive substantial free cash flow acceleration.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9.0% discount rate balances the company's near-monopoly status and recurring revenue in the commercial space against the fierce, expensive competition it faces as it attempts to disrupt entrenched players like Zillow in the residential market.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 3.5% is a robust terminal rate for a digital monopoly. Assuming CoStar successfully cements Homes.com alongside its commercial and multifamily assets, it will benefit from immense, highly scalable, two-sided network effects in perpetuity.

thesisParagraphs: CoStar Group has long dominated the commercial real estate data market, building a proprietary database that functions as an irreplaceable utility for brokers and investors. This core business throws off immense, predictable cash flow.

The true upside lies in its aggressive pivot to the consumer residential market. By heavily investing to scale Homes.com—and leveraging the proven playbook it used with Apartments.com—CoStar is attempting a hostile takeover of a massive new TAM. The current valuation appears to penalize the company excessively for its near-term marketing spend while underestimating the long-term cash flow potential of establishing a dominant, integrated real estate portal.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 15.0% growth rate for CoStar?
faq.answer: Gemini anticipates that the massive upfront marketing investments in Homes.com will eventually taper, while the platform begins to monetize heavily. This, combined with the steady, high-margin growth of its core commercial suite, warrants an aggressive cash flow projection.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for CoStar's DCF?
faq.answer: A 9.0% discount rate was selected. While the commercial business is incredibly stable, the residential expansion carries significant execution risk and requires competing against formidable, well-capitalized incumbents like Zillow.

faq.question: Is it safe to rely on AI for stock valuation?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is a demonstration of AI reasoning based on a specific set of inputs and rigid formulas. It is not financial advice. AI models cannot predict regulatory actions, geopolitical shifts, or black swan economic events.

prospectVerdictDetail: CoStar Group commands a near-monopoly in commercial real estate data and analytics, underpinning its massive moat. The company has aggressively expanded its consumer-facing residential real estate footprint through properties like Apartments.com and Homes.com, successfully challenging legacy portals. With a highly scalable software-as-a-service (SaaS) model and an entrenched position in professional property markets, CoStar's long-term economic prospects are exceptionally strong.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: CoStar exhibits powerful competitive momentum. Its core commercial real estate data business provides a stable, highly profitable base, while its strategic investments in residential portals are yielding rapid market share gains.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: CoStar consistently delivers robust, double-digit revenue growth. Its expansion into the residential sector with Homes.com provides a massive new total addressable market (TAM) to sustain this growth trajectory.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: While already dominant in commercial real estate data, CoStar is actively capturing significant market share in the residential space, positioning Homes.com as a premier alternative to Zillow and Realtor.com.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Within its core CoStar Suite product, the company enjoys immense pricing power. Commercial brokers and investors consider the data mission-critical, allowing for consistent, high-margin subscription price increases.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: The company maintains a strong cadence of feature updates and integrations, particularly focusing on expanding the capabilities and reach of Homes.com and integrating newly acquired properties into its ecosystem.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: CoStar's economic moat is incredibly wide and deep, rooted in a proprietary database built over decades that is practically impossible for a new entrant to replicate.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: For commercial real estate professionals, the CoStar database is deeply embedded in their daily workflows. Switching costs are high because no competitor offers a comparable breadth and depth of historical and active property data.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: Marketplaces like Apartments.com and Homes.com benefit from powerful, two-sided network effects. More listings attract more potential buyers/renters, which in turn attracts more property managers and agents to pay for premium placement.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: CoStar fiercely protects its proprietary database and intellectual property through litigation when necessary. While it faces some scrutiny over its market dominance, its proprietary data collection methods form a strong IP moat.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: As a digital information and marketplace provider, CoStar operates a highly asset-light, scalable business model. The initial cost to build the database was immense, but the incremental cost to serve a new subscriber is negligible, leading to massive capital efficiency.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Investor sentiment is generally positive, focused on the massive long-term potential of the residential real estate expansion, despite the heavy near-term marketing investments required.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Estimates reflect a balance between the highly profitable commercial segment and the significant ongoing marketing spend required to scale Homes.com against entrenched competitors.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative is centered on CoStar's aggressive push into the residential market. News surrounding the expansion of pre-market listings on Homes.com reinforces the company's commitment to disrupting the residential portal space.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Led by founder Andrew Florance, management has a proven track record of executing strategic acquisitions and successfully integrating them to dominate specific real estate verticals.

prospectKeyRisks: Heavy, sustained marketing investments required to build the Homes.com brand could compress overall corporate margins for an extended period.
A severe, prolonged downturn in the commercial real estate market could lead to increased subscriber churn or reduced pricing power for the core CoStar Suite.
Intense competition in the residential portal space from well-capitalized incumbents like Zillow Group.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Homes.com reaching profitability or demonstrating clear agent revenue monetization could re-rate the stock by 30-40% as the market stops valuing the investment as a pure cost center
NAR settlement aftermath restructuring buyer agent economics in ways that favor Homes.com's 'your listing, your lead' model over Zillow's lead-generation approach
CRE market recovery as interest rates normalize could accelerate the core CoStar Suite business to 15%+ growth, funding the residential investment from organic cash flow
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored CSGP at 81/100 and Opus at 58/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
